project: FoodSafe Intelligence — Hebrew University of Jerusalem / Israeli Ministry of Health

model.name: Bi-Encoder Risk Scorer
model.architecture_new: BERT(768) → Dropout(0.3) → Linear(768→100) → ReLU → Dropout(0.3) → Linear(100→1)
changes_per_lev_muchnik: Architecture change reduced test_loss from 1.419 to 0.877

gap_analysis.description: Accuracy broken down by severity gap between the two alerts in each pair

gap_0_same_level.description: Both alerts have same severity (e.g. medium vs medium)

gap_2_far_apart.description: Far apart (e.g. high vs low) — clearest cases

key_findings: Model beats baseline on clear cases (gap=2): 90.6% vs 88.6%
Model adds most value on same-severity pairs (gap=0): 59.3% vs 34.9% baseline
Overfitting observed: train_loss drops each epoch while val_loss rises — best model is epoch 1
Ground truth labels were generated by an LLM (not human experts) — results reflect agreement with LLM, not absolute correctness
limitations: Labels generated by LLM — may contain noise
Severity scales differ across sources (FDA Class I/II/III vs FSA FAFA) — cross-source comparisons are imperfect
